name: Drake Baldwin
headline: Baldwin went 2-for-4 with a walk, a triple, a home run and four RBI in Monday's Grapefruit League game against the Rays.

name: Vladimir Guerrero Jr.
headline: Guerrero went 2-for-2 with a walk, a two-run home run and a second run scored in Sunday's Grapefruit League game against the Rays.

name: Gleyber Torres
headline: Torres was scratched from the lineup for Saturday's Grapefruit League game against the Yankees due to lower-back tightness.

name: Austin Riley
headline: Riley went 3-for-4 with two doubles, a home run and four RBI in Saturday's Grapefruit League game against the Red Sox.

name: Zachary Neto
headline: Neto was removed from Friday's Cactus League game against the White Sox after being hit by a pitch, but he is OK, Rhett Bollinger of MLB.

name: James Wood
headline: Wood went 0-for-2 with a walk and a run scored in Friday's Grapefruit League game against the Blue Jays.

name: Kyle Tucker
headline: Tucker went 2-for-4 with a double and an RBI in Saturday's Grapefruit League game against the Braves.

name: Jac Caglianone
headline: Caglianone went 2-for-3 with a home run and four RBI in Monday's Grapefruit League game against the Rays.

name: Kazuma Okamoto
headline: Okamoto went 1-for-3 with a triple, two RBI and a walk in Friday's World Baseball Classic game against Australia.

name: Pete Crow-Armstrong
headline: Crow-Armstrong went 2-for-3 with a walk and a stolen base in Thursday's Cactus League game against the Angels.

name: Wyatt Langford
headline: Langford went 1-for-3 with a two-run home run and a walk in Wednesday's Cactus League game against the Athletics.

name: Colton Cowser
headline: Cowser went 2-for-3 with a solo home run and a walk in Thursday's Grapefruit League game against the Twins.

name: Jasson Dominguez
headline: Dominguez went 2-for-4 with a solo home run in Saturday's Grapefruit League game against the Mets.

name: Evan Carter
headline: Carter went 2-for-3 with a two-run home run, a walk and three RBI in Wednesday's Cactus League game against the Athletics.

name: Coby Mayo
headline: Mayo went 1-for-4 with a solo home run and two runs scored in Tuesday's Grapefruit League game against the Nationals.

name: Eli Willits
headline: Willits went 3-for-4 with a walk, a run scored, two RBI and a stolen base for Single-A Fredericksburg on Thursday.

name: Charlie Condon
headline: The Rockies reassigned Condon to minor-league camp Thursday.

name: Max Clark
headline: Clark was reassigned to minor-league camp Monday.

name: Tanner Bibee
headline: Bibee allowed eight runs on 10 hits while striking out three over 4.2 innings in Saturday's spring start against San Francisco.

name: MacKenzie Gore
headline: Gore allowed two hits and three walks while striking out three over 4.1 scoreless innings Monday in a 3-2 win over the White Sox.

name: Matthew Liberatore
headline: The Cardinals announced Wednesday that Liberatore will start the team's March 26 season opener against the Rays in St. Louis.

name: Shane McClanahan
headline: McClanahan struck out seven and walked two without allowing a hit or a run in 3.2 innings Sunday against the Pirates.

name: Yoshinobu Yamamoto
headline: Yamamoto tossed five scoreless innings against the Padres in a Cactus League start Friday, allowing three hits and issuing one walk while striking out.

name: Jhoan Duran
headline: Duran threw a clean inning during Friday's Grapefruit League game against the Orioles.

name: Griffin Jax
headline: Jax has given up one run on four hits with seven strikeouts and one walk across 4.2 combined innings between the Grapefruit League and WBC.

name: Mason Miller
headline: Padres manager Craig Stammen confirmed Wednesday that Miller will serve as the team's closer in 2026.

name: Tyler Rogers
headline: Rogers signed a three-year, $37 million contract with the Twins.

name: JoJo Romero
headline: Romero is expected to be among the relievers in the mix to close games for the Cardinals this season.

name: Mick Abel
headline: Abel is poised to begin the season in Minnesota's starting rotation after Zebby Matthews was optioned to Triple-A St. Paul.

name: Jose Soriano
headline: The Angels announced Tuesday that Soriano will start Los Angeles' March 26 season opener against the Astros in Houston.
